summary: Android Editor: Fix embed mode orientation handling

description.text:
Follow up to https://github.com/godotengine/godot/pull/102492, this PR fixes the orientation handling for embedded mode.

Since the game can apply its own orientation, it causes layout issues when its in window mode and orientation is set to portrait. It break the editor layout.

To address this:  
- Orientation changes are now **disabled** when **embed mode** is enabled and running in **windowed mode** (i.e., fullscreen is not active). 
- If the game requests an orientation change in **windowed mode**, it will be **cached**. 
- The cached orientation will be **applied when switching to fullscreen**. 
- When switching back to **windowed mode**, the orientation will **revert to landscape** to maintain editor layout. 

<details><summary>Before this PR</summary>

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/dbeb33ec-266d-4ca6-96da-f07ad9fa024e

</details> 
<details><summary>After this PR</summary>

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/baf31bb6-35a1-4286-8939-c691ba2b69a0

</details> 

It also fixes an issue where tapping the cutout area would unintentionally minimize the game or switch it to Picture-in-Picture mode.

summary: MultiMeshInstance3D Fade Mode Culling doesn't respect AABB

description.text:
### Tested versions

4.3-stable, 4.4-beta3

### System information

Godot v4.3.stable - Windows 10.0.22631 - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce RTX 3070 Laptop GPU (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.6614) - 12th Gen Intel(R) Core(TM) i9-12900H (20 Threads)

### Issue description

Normally, both MeshInstance3Ds and MMIs cull based on comparing the AABB of the visible meshes with the visibility_ranges. 

This means, even if the MMI instance transforms are far from the location of the MMI node, they are still culled based upon distance to the center of the AABBs encompassing the instances. The MMI location is ignored.

However, if `visibility_range_fade_mode = self`, this is not true. Shadows correctly cull based on the AABB. The visual mesh is incorrectly culled by something else, so the shadows show but the mesh does not.

This is preventing us from providing fading between instance LODs in Terrain3D.

MMI location in the center of the instances AABB

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/2ea612ae-8666-40c8-bc86-33f4792d2b74)

MMI location far from the instances AABB

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/f09a11ff-f5e7-44da-a2a2-b22b165ab6ac)

### Steps to reproduce

Open the MRP. Look at TestArea3. Move the camera towards the center of the grey areas. Read the README node for more info.  

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[test-mmi-margin.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/18780067/test-mmi-margin.zip)

summary: Using `Node.duplicate` copies exported custom-class-typed node property pointing to a descendant Node as null

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in: 4.3-stable [77dcf97d8], 4.4-beta3
- Not reproducible in: 4.2.2-stable [15073afe3]

### System information

Windows 11

### Issue description

When you have a node that exports a property of a custom type, and you set that property to point to *any* descendant node, using `Node.duplicate` won't duplicate that property at all, and it will remain `null`.

If you move the descendant node to anywhere else outside the subtree of the tested node, the issue doesn't happen.

If you use the editor to duplicate, the issue doesn't happen.

I guess this is related to work that was made regarding node duplication in recent versions (4.3, 4.4), but the issues they fix don't describe this error, so I figured writing a new issue would be better.

### Steps to reproduce

In the MRP, there's a simple scene setup with 4 nodes (Test > CustomNode > Node > Node2).

CustomNode's `prop` points to Node2. The `Test` node tries to duplicate CustomNode, but `prop` remains null. The error "failed to duplicate" is output to the console.

Reparenting Node2 to Test in the editor makes the error go away.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[duplicate-issue-mrp.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/18804518/duplicate-issue-mrp.zip)

summary: Document the valid range of tile coordinates in TileMapLayer

description.text:
As mentioned in [RocketChat](https://chat.godotengine.org/channel/2d?msg=46EZCsQPNBc39e8dF), `TileMapLayer` converts cell coordinates to 16-bit when serializing data.

https://github.com/godotengine/godot/blob/f418603522ebda0e00dd1e39646ceb42b8b62055/scene/2d/tile_map_layer.cpp#L2867-L2869

This behavior dates back to the Godot Is Open Source commit :) Although it is quite rare to use extremely large tile maps, it is perfectly valid for users to create one in the editor. However, the result of doing so may be unexpected.

So I think it's better to document it and acknowledge it as intended rather than as a bug.

summary: Editor windows difficult or impossible to access in single window mode

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Reproduced in: 4.3, 4.2.2

### System information

macOS

### Issue description

in certain situations where a few subwindows are stacked, it is difficult or impossible to reach one of those windows if it gets hidden behind the others - if you accidentally click another one for example.

### Steps to reproduce

1. have a small screen or small editor window
2. enable single window mode
3. disable native file dialogs
4. open the export menu
5. open one of the file pickers
6. create new folder
    - you should now have 3 embedded subwindows floating around, the create folder one being tiny
8. click one of the larger subwindows, bringing it to front and hiding the tiny window

if you are unlucky, your screen is too small for the larger windows to be moved and resized out of the way (because the subwindows are bound by the editor window size) to get the small create dialog - and it is impossible to interact with the larger ones while that dialog is open

i could think of multiple way that this situation could be avoided
1. allow closing the parent window while the subwindow is still open (file picker while create directory is open, export while both of them are open)
2. allow moving the subwindows out of the main editor window 
3. closing the folder create popup when the file selector regains focus 
    - there may be other similar situations that are missed by this though
3. allow these blocking windows to shrink further, blocking less space. seems very flimsy to me though

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

editor only

summary: For the same skeleton, importing it in the .fbx format works normally, while importing it in the .glb format results in abnormal bones.

description.text:
### Tested versions

Reproducible in: versions 4.3 and 4.4.

### System information

Godot v4.3.stable - Windows 10.0.26100 - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce RTX 3060 Laptop GPU (NVIDIA; 31.0.15.4665) - 11th Gen Intel(R) Core(TM) i5-11320H @ 3.20GHz (8 Threads)

### Issue description

When I applied an animation to my model, I found that the animation was not working correctly. Upon checking the model, I discovered that the skeleton was not quite right. Specifically, the third bone of each finger was missing. Normally, each finger should have three bones, but now only two are present. I initially thought there was an issue with the model itself. However, when I imported the model into Godot using the FBX format from Blender, the skeleton appeared completely normal, confirming that the model was fine.
This led me to suspect that there might be a bug in Blender when exporting to the GLB format. But when I imported the same GLB file into the Unreal Engine, the skeleton was also completely normal. Therefore, it can be concluded that there is a serious bug in Godot's GLB import functionality: the imported skeleton is incorrect.

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/7d8d046f-2aaf-4ff2-945a-ff66d2a22ba7)

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/0619478e-f591-40c7-9193-5946311b7236)

### Steps to reproduce

I quickly generated a 3D character model using Vroid Studio, imported it into Blender, and then imported it into Godot in both GLB and FBX formats. The bones were incorrect when imported in GLB format, but correct when imported in FBX format.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

summary: Improve 3D editor's preview sun and environment UX

description.text:
This PR aims to make some improvements to the preview sun and preview environment (henceforth called preview sun/env) for the 3D editor.
### Project Defaults
Previously, users would have no choice in what preview sun/env were applied to new scenes. This is problematic because art direction and graphics needs differ between projects, so having every scene preview by default with glow and color correction enabled creates error-prone workflows where artists cannot see a correct "neutral". It is also tedious to change every scene by hand. This PR adds project settings which allow the user to edit the default values for preview sun/env settings.

![Screenshot 2025-02-12 021429](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/4435fb17-37ca-4bfc-beb9-b3eb126d1cd0)
### Shadow Toggle
For projects that do not use shadows, it was previously _impossible_ to disable shadows for the preview sun. As before, this is problematic because it does not allow the user to edit props or objects in a correct neutral _for their game_. This PR adds a toggle for shadows.
### Revert Buttons
Previously, it was impossible to quickly revert to default preview settings, meaning the user had to not only remember what the defaults are, but also manually enter them. This PR adds revert buttons at the top of each of the preview sun/env sections.

![Untitled-1](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/710188f0-9001-4abe-b703-7fa6d121f67f)
### Other Fixes
- Previously, when a new scene was created, the last known state of preview sun/env (from whatever other scene was visited last) was carried over into the new scene. This is arbitrary and error-prone. This PR makes it so that new scenes get the default preview settings applied.
- Previously, the glow button state was not always correctly set in some cases. This PR handles them.

summary: Publishing a C# Project via AOT with Spaces in the Project Name Fails

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in: 4.3 stable (mono)

### System information

Arch Linux x64 | gcc 14.2.1, clang 19.1.7, dotnet-sdk/runtime 9.0.1.sdk102

### Issue description

When using NativeAOT export, if the project's name has a space in it, the `publish` command will not quote the `.exports` file, causing compilation to fail.

```bash
MSB3073: The command ""clang" "/home/user/cool-project/.godot/mono/temp/obj/ExportDebug/linux-x64/native/Cool Project.o" -o "/home/user/cool-project/.godot/mono/temp/bin/ExportDebug/linux-x64/native/Cool Project.so" -Wl,--version-script=/home/user/cool-project/.godot/mono/temp/obj/ExportDebug/linux-x64/native/Cool Project.exports -Wl,--export-dynamic -gz=zlib -fuse-ld=bfd /home/user/.nuget/packages/runtime.linux-x64.microsoft.dotnet.ilcompiler/8.0.12/sdk/libbootstrapperdll.o /home/user/.nuget/packages/runtime.linux-x64.microsoft.dotnet.ilcompiler/8.0.12/sdk/libRuntime.WorkstationGC.a /home/user/nuget/packages/runtime.linux-x64.microsoft.dotnet.ilcompiler/8.0.12/sdk/libeventpipe-disabled.a /home/user/.nuget/packages/runtime.linux-x64.microsoft.dotnet.ilcompiler/8.0.12/sdk/libstdc++compat.a /home/user/.nuget/packages/runtime.linux-x64.microsoft.dotnet.ilcompiler/8.0.12/framework/libSystem.Native.a /home/user/.nuget/packages/runtime.linux-x64.microsoft.dotnet.ilcompiler/8.0.12/framework/libSystem.Globalization.Native.a /home/user/.nuget/packages/runtime.linux-x64.microsoft.dotnet.ilcompiler/8.0.12/framework/libSystem.IO.Compression.Native.a /home/user/.nuget/packages/runtime.linux-x64.microsoft.dotnet.ilcompiler/8.0.12/framework/libSystem.Net.Security.Native.a /home/user/.nuget/packages/runtime.linux-x64.microsoft.dotnet.ilcompiler/8.0.12/framework/libSystem.Security.Cryptography.Native.OpenSsl.a -g -Wl,-rpath,'$ORIGIN' -Wl,--build-id=sha1 -Wl,--as-needed -Wl,-e0x0 -pthread -ldl -lz -lrt -lm -shared -Wl,-z,relro -Wl,-z,now -Wl,--eh-frame-hdr -Wl,--discard-all -Wl,--gc-sections" exited with code 1. /home/user/.nuget/packages/microsoft.dotnet.ilcompiler/8.0.12/build/Microsoft.NETCore.Native.targets(366,5)
```

`--version-script=/home/user/cool-project/.godot/mono/temp/obj/ExportDebug/linux-x64/native/Cool Project.exports` is the problem.

The .csproj:

```xml
<Project Sdk="Godot.NET.Sdk/4.3.0">
  <PropertyGroup>
    <TargetFramework>net8.0</TargetFramework>
    <TargetFramework Condition=" '$(GodotTargetPlatform)' == 'android' ">net7.0</TargetFramework>
    <TargetFramework Condition=" '$(GodotTargetPlatform)' == 'ios' ">net8.0</TargetFramework>
    <EnableDynamicLoading>true</EnableDynamicLoading>
    <RootNamespace>CoolProject</RootNamespace>
    <PublishAOT>true</PublishAOT>
  </PropertyGroup>

  <ItemGroup>
    <TrimmerRootAssembly Include="GodotSharp" />
    <TrimmerRootAssembly Include="$(TargetName)" />
  </ItemGroup>
</Project>
```

### Steps to reproduce

1. Create new Godot project with C# enabled, with a space in the project's name
2. Generate .csproj+.sln via editor tools
3. Add `<PublishAOT>true</PublishAOT>` and root block to prevent trimming
4. Create any export preset and try to export, with or without debug

summary: Game and editor freeze when clicking on the game's title bar

description.text:
### Tested versions

reproducible in v4.4.beta1.official [d33da79d3] through v4.4.beta3.official [06acfccf8]
not reproducible in v4.4.dev7.official [46c8f8c5c] and earlier

### System information

Godot v4.4.beta3 - Windows 11 (build 26100) - Multi-window, 2 monitors - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce RTX 3050 (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.6636) - AMD Ryzen 5 5600G with Radeon Graphics (12 threads)

### Issue description

When using the embed game option, touching the title bar of the window containing the game freezes the game and the editor for several seconds (usually just a couple but sometimes it's seemingly permanent)

If the floating workspace option is on, the freeze only happens when interacting with the game window's title bar.

If the floating workspace option is off and the game is embedded directly in the editor's window, the freeze happens when interacting with the editor's title bar.

If embedding is disabled altogether, the title bar is fine and can be interacted with without freezing anything.

The embedded toolbar (the bar under the title bar and above the game) can be interacted with just fine, the same with the game itself.

The resizing of the game's or the editor's window does not cause freezing. Though the game's viewport lags behind the window, which does not happen if embedding is disabled.

The title bar buttons (minimize, maximize, and close) are reacting to hover fine.
Clicking the minimize button seems to work every time without freezing.
Clicking the maximize button works sometimes, but sometimes can cause permanent freeze.
Clicking the close button works sometimes, but sometimes can cause permanent freeze.

Freezing does not seem to increase CPU or memory usage.

This is also independent on the renderer: Forward+, Mobile, and Compatibility are all affected.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Open any project in Godot 4.4 beta 3
2. Make sure the "embed game on next play" is on
3. Run the project
4. Click or drag on the title bar of the window in which the game is embedded
5. The windows freeze 😭

N/A (happens in any project)

summary: Native file dialog selection does not work as expected

description.text:
### Tested versions

🤖 4.3, 4.4 beta2

### System information

🪟 Windows 11

### Issue description

I have "Use Native File Dialogs" enabled, since I prefer the Window dialog by a lot.

However, when picking files for a an option with multiple possible file types (such as sprite sheets), the accepted files setting is on the first type and there's no "All Recognized"/"All Allowed Types" option.
This initially caused me to believe I was doing something out of order and needed to import my .pngs somewhere else / turn them into a spritesheet first.

I think it's a clear standard for applications to default to "All Allowed Types" when picking files and should be the standard for Godot's Native File Dialogs.

Another problem is that extensions need to be explicitly specified in the file dialog. For instance, when saving a resource as my_resource.tres, you need to fully type out 'my_resource**.tres**', otherwise the operation will fail. This happens despite the file extension being specified in the save dialog.

I believe this violates a universal standard of automatically extending the filename with the extension which I'm used to from other applications.

### Steps to reproduce

Enable "Use Native File Dialogs" in the Editor Settings.

Create a Sprite Frames Resource and click the "From Sprite Sheet" Button.

The file selection is on *.tres instead of "All Allowed Types", hiding .pngs and other possible file types.

---

Create a new resource in the Editor.

Try to save it as "my_resource" in the native file dialog (with the type field showing .tres).

The dialog will close, but the Engine will state that the operation failed.

summary: Xbox 1708 Controller connected over Bluetooth is mapped to 2 input devices

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in 3.6 and 4.3
- Last relevant change to "platform/windows/joypad_windows.cpp" in commit: 81f9c0345c963ab1f06c2ed1d2d76368bdbd8f56

### System information

Godot v4.4.beta3 - Windows 11 (build 26100) - Multi-window, 1 monitor - OpenGL 3 (Compatibility) - NVIDIA GeForce RTX 4070 Laptop GPU (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.6636) - Intel(R) Core(TM) Ultra 9 185H (22 threads)

### Issue description

If the Xbox 1708 controller is connected with USB it's always recognized as a __XINPUT_DEVICE__ and behaves as expected irrelevant of the used firmware version.

With a downgraded firmware version to 3.1.1221.0 and connected over Bluetooth it is recognized as a "Bluetooth XINPUT-compatible input device" and is working as expected as well.

However, if the firmware is updated to the latest version 5.23.5.0 and connected over Bluetooth it is now mapped as two devices.
- Bluetooth LE XINPUT compatible input device - 030000005e040000200b000000000000
- __XINPUT_DEVICE__

In 2021 Microsoft added Bluetooth LE support to this controller and it looks like that change is not correctly reflected in Godot.

There is a closed PR https://github.com/godotengine/godot/pull/89587 which adresses the issue and if I recompile Godot with these changes only 1 "Bluetooth LE XINPUT compatible input device" is detected. Sadly, I don't have the other ones to verify the rest of the changes.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Update Controller to latest firmware (5.23.5.0)
2. Connect controller over Bluetooth
3. Run the AssetLibrary project "Joypads (Gamepads) Demo"
4. Check console output

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

Use "Joypads (Gamepads) Demo" from the AssetLibrary

summary: AudioEffectCapture Produces Distorted Output on 44.1 kHz Input Systems

description.text:
### Tested versions


- Reproducible in 4.2.stable, 4.4.beta3

### System information

Windows 10 - 44100 kHz Input Audio

### Issue description

 I have a bus layout with 3 buses, Master, Muted, Record. Record has a AudioEffectCapture in it and trasfers its audio into Muted which is indeed muted. Then I have an AudioStreamPlayer with an AudioStreamMicrophone. 

I press an action (via the input map) to activate and deactivate (play or not play) the Stream in AudioStreamMicrophone. Then, via code I get the buffer like this:

```
while _recording:
    var start_time = Time.get_ticks_msec()
        
    # Get new frames from the capture.
    var chunk = _effect_capture.get_buffer(_effect_capture.get_frames_available())
    if chunk.size() > 0:
        _accumulated_frames.append_array(chunk)
```

I then save the audio (_accumulated_frames) into a .wav file at the sample rate AudioServer.get_mix_rate(). In systems with 48 kHz input audio this works perfectly. In systems with 44.1 kHz input audio the .wav originated from _accumulated_frames sounds like the audio file attached.

[debug_acc_frames.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/18747747/debug_acc_frames.zip)

On systems with a 48 kHz input audio rate, the generated .wav file plays back correctly and my posterior speech to text scripts work correctly.
On systems with a 44.1 kHz input audio rate, the resulting .wav file sounds distorted and my posterior speech to text scripts break, see the attached audio file.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Set up an AudioStreamMicrophone routed to a bus containing AudioEffectCapture.
2. Capture audio frames using _effect_capture.get_buffer().
3. Save the recorded audio as a .wav file using AudioServer.get_mix_rate() for the sample rate.
4. Test on a system with 44.1 kHz input audio and listen to the resulting .wav file, it will be stuttery and in slow motion.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

See above

summary: Binary and Hexadecimal literals cannot include a separator underscore between literal and the number

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in 4.1-beta3, 4.3-stable, 4.4-beta2
- Not Reproducible in 4.1-beta2

- Should additionally be reproducible in all published versions after v4.1-beta3 as of this issue being posted

This behaviour changed in commit fba8cbe6dbf17399e06ac9141a862734187dfb65, as part of pr <https://github.com/godotengine/godot/pull/73203> 

### System information

Godot v4.1.beta3 - macOS 15.3.0 - Vulkan (Compatibility) - Apple M1 - Apple M1 (8 Threads)

### Issue description

When writing a binary or hexadecimal literal, an underscore cannot be used between the letter indicator (`0b` or `0x`) and the number

This is a very minor issue, and can be bypassed by simply putting a 0 before the first underscore, but personally i feel that `0b_0001` is clearer than `0b0_0001`, and running the provided project in 4.1-beta2 or prior  parses everything correctly.


```gdscript
extends Node

func _ready() -> void:
	print("Binary Literals")
	print(0b0001) # 1
	print(0b_0001) # 1, errors
	print(0b_0000_0001) # 1, errors
	print(0b0000_0001_) # 1
	
	print("\nHex Literals")
	print(0xF) # 15
	print(0x_F) # 15, errors
	print(0x_F_) # 15, errors
	print(0x0_F) # 15
	
	print("\nDecimal Literals")
	print(1_000.200_300) # 1000.2003
	print(1000.200300) # 1000.2003
	print(1_000.200_300) # 1000.2003
```

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/54d6615d-740e-4285-812c-8391509f3550)

### Steps to reproduce

In any script in editor version 4.1-beta3 or later, create a binary or hexadecimal literal with an underscore directly after the indicator letter, followed by a valid number for that format. The script will fail to parse, with the reason `Unexpected underscore after "0b".` (or `0x` for hex)

```gdscript
print(0b0001) # 1
print(0b_0001) # 1, error
print(0b_0000_0001) # 1, error
print(0b0000_0001) # 1
print(0b0000_0001_) # 1
 
print(0xF) # 15
print(0x_F) # 15, error
print(0x_0_F) # 15, error
print(0x0_F) #15
print(0x0_F_) # 15
```

Opening the minimal reproduction project and opening the included script, `binary_int_testing.gd`, will show the errors seen in the image included in the issue description

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[MRP_binary_literal.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/18747216/MRP_binary_literal.zip)

summary: LightmapGI baking does not work on Apple Silicon Mac when the project renderer is Compatibility

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in: v4.4.beta3
- Not reproducible in: v4.3.stable

### System information

Godot v4.4.beta3 - macOS Sonoma (14.5.0) - Multi-window, 1 monitor - Metal (Mobile) - integrated Apple M1 (Apple7) - Apple M1 (8 threads)

### Issue description

Upon doing LightmapGI baking on my Apple Silicon Mac while the project is on Compatibility renderer, this shows up.

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/ea012bb1-e6b6-49fe-b173-70cbbdada145)

and here's the error.
```
  ERROR: drivers/gles3/storage/texture_storage.cpp:763 - Condition "t->is_render_target" is true.
  ERROR: Failed loading resource: res://lightmap_gi.exr. Make sure resources have been imported by opening the project in the editor at least once.
  ERROR: scene/3d/lightmap_gi.cpp:878 - Condition "t.is_null()" is true. Returning: LightmapGI::BAKE_ERROR_CANT_CREATE_IMAGE
  ERROR: scene/3d/lightmap_gi.cpp:1293 - Condition "save_err != BAKE_ERROR_OK" is true. Returning: save_err
```

Switching the project to either Mobile or Forward+ won't show this error, and baking works normally.
However this is not a normal behavior since baking works normally on Compatibility render on Linux. (My device is Ubuntu 20.04)

---

In addition, opening said baked lightmap back to Compatibility will result in error.

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/456cc402-19b6-410c-9afa-e8b7aed1310e)

That error shows up, and crashed my engine. (This one was not the MRP)

Full crash log: https://pastebin.com/7hLrbfii

### Steps to reproduce

- Download and open the MRP project
- Open lightmap_gi.tscn
- Make sure currently the renderer (top right) is Mobile)
- Click on the LightmapGI node, then click Bake Lightmap
- Save the bake file anywhere

At this point, baking should happen normally.
- remove the lmbake and exr file from the filesystem dock
- reset the light data property of the LightmapGI node
- change renderer to Compatibility, and let the project restart
- repeat the same step above to bake
- notice that now the baking is failed with errors

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[mac-lightmap-bug.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/18746707/mac-lightmap-bug.zip)

summary: Improve path handling in EditorQuickOpenDialog

description.text:
- Store path history as UIDs
  - Allows "auto-update" paths when files are moved, so your history items won't break
- Support UID paths in the dialog
- When loading history, remove files that no longer exist
  - Only happens at first opening. If a file is gone between openings and you try to open wrong file, error dialog appears and the entry is deleted

I also added `get_file_uid()` method to EditorFileSystem, which is faster than going through ResourceLoader. We should use it from now where applicable.

summary: "Attempted to free invalid ID" errors are printed when exiting a project run with Debug Canvas Item Redraw

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in: 4.4.beta2, 4.4.beta2
- Not reproducible in: 4.3.stable, 4.4.beta1

### System information

Godot v4.4.beta (36d90c73a) - Fedora Linux 41 (KDE Plasma) on X11 - X11 display driver, Multi-window, 1 monitor - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce RTX 4090 (nvidia; 565.77) - 13th Gen Intel(R) Core(TM) i9-13900K (32 threads)

### Issue description

Running the MRP with `--debug-canvas-item-redraw` will result in the following on exit:

```
ERROR: Attempted to free invalid ID: 4509715660816
   at: _free_internal (./servers/rendering/rendering_device.cpp:6106)
ERROR: Attempted to free invalid ID: 4526895530004
   at: _free_internal (./servers/rendering/rendering_device.cpp:6106)
ERROR: Attempted to free invalid ID: 4539780431895
   at: _free_internal (./servers/rendering/rendering_device.cpp:6106)
```

The invalid IDs are the usually same on every run (in this particular project), especially the first one. No further information about this is printed when using `--verbose`.

The feature still works correctly, so it's not a big issue but we should still aim to fix it.

I've bisected this to https://github.com/godotengine/godot/pull/101931. cc @stuartcarnie 

### Steps to reproduce

- Add a CheckButton node.
- With the editor being run from a terminal (so you can see all errors on exit), enable **Debug > Visible Canvas Item Redraw** or run the project with `--debug-canvas-item-redraw` when using Forward+ or Mobile.
- Exit the project and notice errors being printed in the terminal.

summary: Game freeze for several seconds when adding a certain 3D scene to the SceneTree on low-end Android device (Compatibility)

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in: 4.4.beta3, 4.4.beta2, 4.3.stable, v4.4.beta.custom_build [b70f2b9b4] ([shader-baker](https://github.com/DarioSamo/godot/tree/shader-baker))
- Also reproducible (but not as severe) in: 4.2.2.stable, 4.0.4.stable

### System information

Godot v4.3.stable - Windows 10.0.22631 - GLES3 (Compatibility) - AMD Radeon(TM) Graphics (Advanced Micro Devices, Inc.; 32.0.12033.1030) - AMD Ryzen 5 6600H with Radeon Graphics (12 Threads)

### Issue description

**TLDR:** The game freeze for 5-8 seconds on the loading screen while the next level's music plays. Only happens the first time said level is accessed since install.

**Main Problem:**
The game freeze when calling add_child() with an instantiated simple 3D scene as the parameter. The 3D scene's _ready() function is called despite the scene hasn't showed up yet on the screen. More noticable on low-end Android device (see below). With the MRP and said device, the freeze lasts for 5 seconds. This happens only the first time when the game installed and launched. Going back to said scene after that, or closing and reopening the app then accessing said scene is significantly faster.
Upon inspection, the freeze happens because it took RenderingServer a while to update the Viewports.
Tested on Compatibility renderer.

**Android device used:**
Xiaomi Redmi 9C | 3GB (2772MB) RAM | CPU Mediatek Helio P35 (MT6765G) | GPU PowerVR GE8320 (Rogue) @ 680 MHz | OpenGL ES 3.2  | 64-bit ARMv8-A (32-bit Mode) | Released on 2020

**Demonstration:**
(click below to play the video on a new tab, idk why this isn't embedded)

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/d7989e79-0485-4d48-8ac0-26ac228389d4

**Explanation:**
The MRP (where I made it as close as possible to my game) has a custom scene switching method. It first load a scene with a root node named "SubRoot" as the scenetree's current scene. Then this node will handle scene switching with ResourceLoader.load_threaded to prevent lag. SubRoot has a child named "GameRoot", which subsequent scene change will be added as a (sole) child to this, During the change, a loading screen (Control node) will cover the GameRoot node until the load-instantiation-add_child process is complete, then hide the loading screen.

In practice, using the device specified above will experience the problem.

On the game scene, I've provided several (probably) useful statistics to help investigate the issue. They are timers made with comparing Time.get_ticks_usec()

Here is the scene loaded on my laptop's editor (like the one i provided on the System Information section)

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/6ea8a18a-8890-4039-8f0b-33d8b2a0e5ce)

The freeze or delay is roughly 0.3 seconds, but we could say it's justified/forgivable/barely noticeable. My other desktop device has only 0.5 seconds of delay. Now for the statistics:

- _"Instantiation Delay"_ : duration between the moment the loaded scene is instantiated until the _ready() function of said scene is executed (meaning in theory the scene is already on the scenetree, and _should_ appear visible by user)
- _"Scene show delay"_ : duration between the moment the loaded scene is instantiated until the loading screen is hidden (when the next scene finished being added), timed by using visibility_changed signal. The value will update about 8 seconds after the delay to compensate with the freeze. This value could have different result whether threading is used for the scene instantiation. Threads usage is used by default but could also be deactivated by changing an exported boolean property on Subroot's Inspector.
- _"RenderingServer update delay"_ : duration between the moment the loaded scene is instantiated until the RenderingServer has finished updating all the Viewports (detected using frame_post_draw signal). This and the stat below are approximately the duration of the freeze.
-  _"RenderingServer update duration"_ : duration in which RenderingServer updates all viewports, calculated from frame_pre_draw and frame_post_draw signal.

Now here's the result when i use the Redmi 9C i mentioned above, with their respective Godot version used to export the apk:

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/3a82607f-e98e-42af-aac8-4ce020ea99e9)
As you can see there's a significant delay by RenderingServer detected from the stats, and it roughly matches the screen freeze duration on the device. It only happens the first time loading this scene since the app is installed on the device (and on subsequent installation if the previous app data is cleared).

**Assumptions:**
Before, I thought this was an instantiation issue, but after experimenting more with the timer, it seems that the add_child() method is the one causing the lag/freeze. However, since the music plays while the screen remains frozen on the loading screen, it's safe to assume that the add_child() method is successful. The issue appears to be with the RenderingServer, which needs more time to update the viewport.
This is just my assumption, i don't even understand how RenderingServer works.
Some people says it's the shader compilation, which could make sense since it only happens the first time the level is loaded,

**Additional Note:**
- I haven't tested 4.1.stable
- Haven't tested with Vulkan. My device has Vulkan support but it couldn't run any 3D scene with it (which is a whole another issue)
- I use threads on the code because someone tells me to test if the problem still persist if threads is used. After testing, it still does, but with slightly different freeze duration. I don't know if i wrote the thread related code correctly though, feel free to modify.
- With threads on, "Scene show delay" shows the _supposed_ time needed from the instantiation to when the scene shows up. Meanwhile with threads off, "Scene show delay" shows the _actual_ time wasted, with the freeze included.
- Godot 4.2 and below tested with threads disabled since the same exact code is not threads safe back then apparently (and causes error).
- The freeze duration difference on Godot 4.2 and below probably because the difference in mesh importer system. Though i tested using glb file of the same mesh and the freeze still exist.
- I tested on a mid end device, Redmi 9 (both released on 2020 from the same lineup), the freeze is just about 1.5-2 seconds. So probably the freeze is more apparent on lower end devices.
- There are probably other issues similar to this, but i made this issue anyway because the outcome is specific
- I know about the 4.4 ubershader update but idk if it affects this. I removed the sky shader from the MRP to minimize the usage of shader.
- I tried having shader baker turned on when exporting with a build from shader-baker branch out of curiosity, not much changed.

**Probably Similar/Related issues:**
- https://www.reddit.com/r/godot/comments/17rlyl3/two_scene_loader_issues_global_freeze_for_some/

**Why is this a problem:**
From a user’s perspective, an unresponsive game is a big deal, especially on mobile devices. Sure, most of the loading could be hidden behind a loading screen, but this freeze bug kicks in right after the screen goes away. Ideally, the process causing this delay should still be tucked away behind the loading screen. But currently, there are no methods to do so.

Even if this only happens the first time a level loads, it’s still an issue. In my project (not the MRP), a scene with the same 3D ground but with characters takes about 8–10 seconds to load, and that delay could easily scale with more complex content.

What’s more, the freeze stops the AnimationSprite2D (the loading circle), making the game look completely unresponsive. Would be nice if we could set priority to some node to still works during whatever caused the freeze. (I tried adjusting the process_priority, but that didn’t help at the time.) Even if the freeze only occurs once, that initial experience matters for user. I couldn’t simply move all the loading to the start screen because having the game stuck on the initial screen for a long time is even worse. Users might not tolerate an extended wait on the splash screen.
I know this might sound like a feature request, but since it’s caused by a bug, I’m reporting it here. With the Compatibility renderer aimed at low-end devices, it’s important to figure out the solution to this for a smoother experience on older hardware.

### Steps to reproduce

- Download the MRP and open the project in Godot (4.4 or 4.3 preferred)
- In Editor > Editor Settings > Export > Android, set One Click Deploy Clear Previous Install to true/active
![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/183827fc-22a8-49fc-9359-a8a85fcee5a2)
- Make sure you installed the required Export Templates and dependencies for Android export
- Prepare a low end Android device (armv7 32-bit preferred)
- Connect the Android device to the desktop and install the app via One Click Deploy (or install via apk but remember to clear data and uninstall before reinstallation)
- Open the game:
- wait for the first loading screen
- when a "Play Game" button shows up, click it
- take a look at the second loading screen
- notice how it get stuck on 99/100%, the rotating circle stops, and a music play in the background, indicating the next scene is already there but the visible screen is still on the loading screen.
- when the scene finally loads, check on the statistics
- try going back to main menu, and play again. Notice how the load time is significantly faster.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[instancing-bugtest.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/18734187/instancing-bugtest.zip)
